---
title: "COLORECTAL NORM-FIRST"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# LCP1-NET: WGCNA Pipeline — Strategy 2
# Within-dataset CPM normalisation before combination
#
# Comparison pipeline against Strategy 1
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── Load libraries ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(ggplot2)
library(jsonlite)
library(limma)
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
    library(recount3)
    library(DESeq2)
    library(WGCNA)
    library(flashClust)
    library(SummarizedExperiment)
    library(gridExtra)
})

allowWGCNAThreads()

# ── Paths ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BASE_PATH_S2 <- "~/Desktop/COLORECTAL CANCER/LCP1-NET/strategy2"

dir.create(file.path(BASE_PATH_S2, "data/wgcna"),     recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(BASE_PATH_S2, "data/processed"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(BASE_PATH_S2, "ml_outputs"),     recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(BASE_PATH_S2, "plots"),          recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

LCP1_ENSEMBL <- "ENSG00000159111"

cat("Strategy 2 environment ready ✓\n")
cat(rep("=", 60), "\n", sep="")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 1 — DATA ACQUISITION
# Identical to Strategy 1 — reuse saved data if already downloaded
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\nBLOCK 1: Data Acquisition\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

# ── Option A: Reload from Strategy 1 save (recommended) ──────────────
# If Strategy 1 already ran, reload the saved raw counts
# This saves time and ensures both strategies use identical input data

s1_raw_path <- "~/Desktop/COLORECTAL CANCER/LCP1-NET/all_raw_counts.RData"

if (file.exists(s1_raw_path)) {
    cat("Loading raw counts from Strategy 1 save...\n")
    load(s1_raw_path)
    cat("Loaded: counts_colon, normal_coad_counts, normal_read_counts\n")
    cat("Loaded: tumour_coad_counts, tumour_read_counts\n")
    cat("Loaded: meta_colon, meta_coad, meta_read\n")
    cat("Strategy 1 raw data reused ✓\n")

} else {
    # ── Option B: Re-download fresh ───────────────────────────────────
    cat("Strategy 1 save not found — downloading fresh...\n")

    set_recount3_url <- function() {
        urls <- c(
            "https://recount-opendata.s3.amazonaws.com/recount3/release",
            "https://sciserver.org/public-data/recount3/data",
            "http://duffel.rail.bio/recount3"
        )
        for (url in urls) {
            cat("Trying:", url, "... ")
            tryCatch({
                options(recount3_url = url)
                test <- available_projects()
                cat("OK ✓\n")
                return(invisible(test))
            }, error = function(e) cat("FAILED\n"))
        }
        stop("All recount3 URLs failed.")
    }

    human_projects <- set_recount3_url()

    # GTEx Colon
    rse_colon    <- create_rse(subset(human_projects,
        project == "COLON" & file_source == "gtex"))
    counts_colon <- assay(rse_colon, "raw_counts")
    meta_colon   <- colData(rse_colon)

    # TCGA-COAD
    rse_coad    <- create_rse(subset(human_projects,
        project == "COAD" & file_source == "tcga" &
        project_type == "data_sources"))
    counts_coad <- assay(rse_coad, "raw_counts")
    meta_coad   <- colData(rse_coad)

    tcga_barcodes_coad <- meta_coad$tcga.tcga_barcode
    sample_type_coad   <- substr(tcga_barcodes_coad, 14, 15)
    tumour_coad_counts <- counts_coad[, sample_type_coad == "01"]
    normal_coad_counts <- counts_coad[, sample_type_coad == "11"]

    # TCGA-READ
    rse_read    <- create_rse(subset(human_projects,
        project == "READ" & file_source == "tcga" &
        project_type == "data_sources"))
    counts_read <- assay(rse_read, "raw_counts")
    meta_read   <- colData(rse_read)

    tcga_barcodes_read <- meta_read$tcga.tcga_barcode
    sample_type_read   <- substr(tcga_barcodes_read, 14, 15)
    tumour_read_counts <- counts_read[, sample_type_read == "01"]
    normal_read_counts <- counts_read[, sample_type_read == "11"]
}

n_colon <- ncol(counts_colon)
n_coad  <- ncol(normal_coad_counts)
n_read  <- ncol(normal_read_counts)

cat("\nData Acquisition Summary\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")
cat(sprintf("  %-32s %d\n", "GTEx Colon (normal):",      n_colon))
cat(sprintf("  %-32s %d\n", "TCGA-COAD matched normal:", n_coad))
cat(sprintf("  %-32s %d\n", "TCGA-READ matched normal:", n_read))
cat(sprintf("  %-32s %d\n", "Total normal reference:",
            n_colon + n_coad + n_read))
cat("Block 1 complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 2 — GENE ID HARMONISATION
# Strip ENSEMBL version suffixes — identical to Strategy 1
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\nBLOCK 2: Gene ID Harmonisation\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

strip_version <- function(ids) sub("\\..*", "", ids)

rownames(counts_colon)       <- strip_version(rownames(counts_colon))
rownames(normal_coad_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(normal_coad_counts))
rownames(normal_read_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(normal_read_counts))
rownames(tumour_coad_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(tumour_coad_counts))
rownames(tumour_read_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(tumour_read_counts))

cat("ENSEMBL version suffixes stripped ✓\n")
cat("Example:", head(rownames(counts_colon), 2), "\n")

# Find common genes
common_genes <- Reduce(intersect, list(
    rownames(counts_colon),
    rownames(normal_coad_counts),
    rownames(normal_read_counts),
    rownames(tumour_coad_counts),
    rownames(tumour_read_counts)
))
cat("Common genes across all sources:", length(common_genes), "\n")

# Subset to common genes
counts_colon         <- counts_colon[common_genes, ]
normal_coad_counts   <- normal_coad_counts[common_genes, ]
normal_read_counts   <- normal_read_counts[common_genes, ]
tumour_coad_counts   <- tumour_coad_counts[common_genes, ]
tumour_read_counts   <- tumour_read_counts[common_genes, ]

cat("LCP1 in common genes:", LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% common_genes, "\n")
cat("Block 2 complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 3 — WITHIN-DATASET CPM NORMALISATION BEFORE COMBINING
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# KEY DIFFERENCE FROM STRATEGY 1:
#   Strategy 1: combine raw counts → normalise together
#   Strategy 2: normalise each dataset separately → combine
#
# Rationale:
#   Each dataset has its own library size distribution
#   shaped by its sequencing protocol and depth.
#   Normalising to CPM within each source first removes
#   within-dataset depth differences before cross-dataset
#   batch effects are addressed. This gives limma a cleaner
#   signal to correct in the next step.
#
# CPM = counts per million
# log2(CPM+1) = log-transformed CPM with pseudocount
#   stabilises variance without requiring VST
#   makes data approximately normally distributed
#   removes the integer overflow problem entirely

cat("\nBLOCK 3: Within-dataset CPM normalisation\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

# ── CPM function ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
compute_cpm <- function(counts_matrix) {
    lib_sizes <- colSums(counts_matrix)
    sweep(counts_matrix, 2, lib_sizes, FUN = "/") * 1e6
}

# ── Normalise each source independently ───────────────────────────────
cat("Computing CPM for each source independently...\n")

cpm_colon <- compute_cpm(counts_colon)
cpm_coad  <- compute_cpm(normal_coad_counts)
cpm_read  <- compute_cpm(normal_read_counts)

# Verify all columns sum to 1,000,000
cat("\nColumn sum verification (all should be 1,000,000):\n")
cat("  GTEx Colon mean:", round(mean(colSums(cpm_colon))), "\n")
cat("  TCGA COAD mean: ", round(mean(colSums(cpm_coad))),  "\n")
cat("  TCGA READ mean: ", round(mean(colSums(cpm_read))),  "\n")

# ── Combine CPM-normalised matrices ───────────────────────────────────
normal_combined_cpm <- cbind(cpm_colon, cpm_coad, cpm_read)

cat("\nCombined CPM matrix:", nrow(normal_combined_cpm),
    "genes ×", ncol(normal_combined_cpm), "samples\n")

# ── log2(CPM+1) transformation ────────────────────────────────────────
# Stabilises variance and makes data suitable for limma
# +1 pseudocount prevents log(0) for zero-count genes
normal_combined_log2cpm <- log2(normal_combined_cpm + 1)

cat("log2(CPM+1) transformation applied ✓\n")
cat("Value range: [",
    round(min(normal_combined_log2cpm), 2), ",",
    round(max(normal_combined_log2cpm), 2), "]\n")
# Expected: 0 to approximately 15-18

# ── Batch and tissue labels ───────────────────────────────────────────
# Identical structure to Strategy 1
batch_labels <- c(
    rep("GTEx_Colon",       n_colon),
    rep("TCGA_COAD_Normal", n_coad),
    rep("TCGA_READ_Normal", n_read)
)
tissue_labels <- c(
    rep("colon_gtex",            n_colon),
    rep("colon_matched_normal",  n_coad),
    rep("rectum_matched_normal", n_read)
)
source_labels <- c(
    rep("GTEx", n_colon),
    rep("TCGA", n_coad + n_read)
)

cat("\nBatch distribution:\n")
print(table(batch_labels))

# ── PCA immediately after CPM — before any batch correction ──────────
# This shows baseline separation on a fair scale
cat("\nComputing PCA on log2(CPM+1) combined data...\n")
pca_cpm <- prcomp(t(normal_combined_log2cpm), scale. = FALSE)
var_cpm <- pca_cpm$sdev^2 / sum(pca_cpm$sdev^2) * 100

pca_cpm_df <- data.frame(
    PC1    = pca_cpm$x[, 1],
    PC2    = pca_cpm$x[, 2],
    batch  = batch_labels,
    source = source_labels
)

p_cpm_raw <- ggplot(pca_cpm_df,
                    aes(PC1, PC2, colour = batch, shape = source)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    labs(
        title    = "Strategy 2 — PCA: log2(CPM+1) before batch correction",
        subtitle = paste0("PC1 = ", round(var_cpm[1], 1),
                          "% — compare this to Strategy 1 before-correction PC1"),
        x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_cpm[1], 1), "% variance)"),
        y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_cpm[2], 1), "% variance)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_cpm_raw)

cat("\nSave this PC1 value for comparison with Strategy 1\n")
cat("Strategy 2 pre-correction PC1:", round(var_cpm[1], 1), "%\n")
cat("Block 3 complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 4 — QUALITY CONTROL ON log2CPM MATRIX
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Filter on log2CPM values rather than raw counts
# log2CPM > 1 ≈ CPM > 2 ≈ a gene expressed at meaningful level
# 30% sample threshold + top 15,000 variance filter

cat("\nBLOCK 4: Quality Control\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

# ── Expression filter ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Keep genes with log2CPM > 1 in at least 30% of samples
min_samples_threshold <- ceiling(ncol(normal_combined_log2cpm) * 0.30)
keep_expressed        <- rowSums(normal_combined_log2cpm > 1) >= min_samples_threshold

cat("Genes before filtering:   ", nrow(normal_combined_log2cpm), "\n")
cat("30% threshold:            ", min_samples_threshold, "samples\n")
cat("Genes passing filter:     ", sum(keep_expressed), "\n")

# Check LCP1
lcp1_expressed <- LCP1_ENSEMBL %in%
    rownames(normal_combined_log2cpm)[keep_expressed]
cat("LCP1 at 30% threshold:    ", lcp1_expressed, "\n")

if (!lcp1_expressed) {
    cat("Lowering to 10% to retain LCP1...\n")
    min_samples_threshold <- ceiling(ncol(normal_combined_log2cpm) * 0.10)
    keep_expressed        <- rowSums(normal_combined_log2cpm > 1) >= min_samples_threshold
    cat("Genes at 10%:", sum(keep_expressed), "\n")
    cat("LCP1 at 10%:", LCP1_ENSEMBL %in%
        rownames(normal_combined_log2cpm)[keep_expressed], "\n")
}

# ── Variance filter ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Top 15,000 most variable genes from those passing expression filter
expressed_genes <- rownames(normal_combined_log2cpm)[keep_expressed]
gene_var        <- apply(normal_combined_log2cpm[expressed_genes, ], 1, var)
top_var_flag    <- rank(-gene_var) <= 15000

# Force LCP1 inclusion regardless of variance rank
if (LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% names(gene_var)) {
    top_var_flag[names(gene_var) == LCP1_ENSEMBL] <- TRUE
    cat("LCP1 forced into final gene set ✓\n")
}

keep_final_genes      <- expressed_genes[top_var_flag]
normal_filtered_s2    <- normal_combined_log2cpm[keep_final_genes, ]

cat("\nGenes after variance filter: ", length(keep_final_genes), "\n")
cat("LCP1 in final set:           ",
    LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% rownames(normal_filtered_s2), "\n")

# ── Sample quality check ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# Check on raw counts for the same samples
raw_combined_check <- cbind(counts_colon, normal_coad_counts, normal_read_counts)
lib_sizes          <- colSums(raw_combined_check[keep_final_genes, ])
low_lib_flag       <- lib_sizes < quantile(lib_sizes, 0.05)

if (any(low_lib_flag)) {
    cat("\nRemoving", sum(low_lib_flag), "low-quality samples\n")
    normal_filtered_s2 <- normal_filtered_s2[, !low_lib_flag]
    batch_labels       <- batch_labels[!low_lib_flag]
    tissue_labels      <- tissue_labels[!low_lib_flag]
    source_labels      <- source_labels[!low_lib_flag]
} else {
    cat("All samples pass library size QC ✓\n")
}

cat("\nFinal reference matrix (Strategy 2):\n")
cat("  Genes:       ", nrow(normal_filtered_s2), "\n")
cat("  Samples:     ", ncol(normal_filtered_s2), "\n")
cat("  LCP1 present:", LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% rownames(normal_filtered_s2), "\n")
cat("Block 4 complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 6 — BATCH CORRECTION via limma on log2(CPM+1)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Fix A and Block 5 are NOT needed in Strategy 2:
#   - Fix A: integer overflow cannot occur on log2CPM values
#   - Block 5: VST is replaced by log2(CPM+1) from Block 3
#
# limma::removeBatchEffect applied directly to log2CPM values
# Output: expr_corrected_s2 — ready for WGCNA

cat("\nBLOCK 6: Batch Correction via limma\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

# ── PCA before correction ─────────────────────────────────────────────
pca_before <- prcomp(t(normal_filtered_s2), scale. = FALSE)
var_before <- pca_before$sdev^2 / sum(pca_before$sdev^2) * 100

pca_before_df <- data.frame(
    PC1    = pca_before$x[, 1],
    PC2    = pca_before$x[, 2],
    batch  = batch_labels,
    tissue = tissue_labels,
    source = source_labels
)

p_before <- ggplot(pca_before_df,
                   aes(PC1, PC2, colour = batch, shape = source)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    labs(
        title    = "Strategy 2 — PCA before limma correction",
        subtitle = paste0("PC1 = ", round(var_before[1], 1),
                          "% (filtered log2CPM)"),
        x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_before[1], 1), "% variance)"),
        y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_before[2], 1), "% variance)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_before)

# ── Apply limma batch correction ──────────────────────────────────────
cat("\nApplying limma::removeBatchEffect...\n")
cat("Batches:", paste(unique(batch_labels), collapse = ", "), "\n")

expr_corrected_s2 <- removeBatchEffect(
    normal_filtered_s2,
    batch = batch_labels
)

cat("limma correction complete ✓\n")
cat("Dimensions:", nrow(expr_corrected_s2), "genes ×",
    ncol(expr_corrected_s2), "samples\n")
cat("Value range: [",
    round(min(expr_corrected_s2), 2), ",",
    round(max(expr_corrected_s2), 2), "]\n")

# ── PCA after correction ──────────────────────────────────────────────
pca_after  <- prcomp(t(expr_corrected_s2), scale. = FALSE)
var_after  <- pca_after$sdev^2 / sum(pca_after$sdev^2) * 100

pca_after_df <- data.frame(
    PC1    = pca_after$x[, 1],
    PC2    = pca_after$x[, 2],
    batch  = batch_labels,
    tissue = tissue_labels,
    source = source_labels
)

p_after <- ggplot(pca_after_df,
                  aes(PC1, PC2, colour = batch, shape = source)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    labs(
        title    = "Strategy 2 — PCA after limma correction",
        subtitle = paste0("PC1 = ", round(var_after[1], 1),
                          "% — compare to Strategy 1 after-correction PC1"),
        x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_after[1], 1), "% variance)"),
        y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_after[2], 1), "% variance)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_after)

# ── Save PCA comparison plots ─────────────────────────────────────────
pdf(file.path(BASE_PATH_S2, "plots/strategy2_batch_correction_PCA.pdf"),
    width = 16, height = 7)
grid.arrange(p_before, p_after, ncol = 2)
dev.off()
cat("PCA plots saved\n")

# ── Residual batch correlation diagnostic ─────────────────────────────
batch_numeric  <- as.numeric(factor(batch_labels))
pc1_r          <- cor(pca_after$x[, 1], batch_numeric)
pc2_r          <- cor(pca_after$x[, 2], batch_numeric)

cat("\nResidual batch correlation (Strategy 2):\n")
cat("  PC1 vs batch: ", round(pc1_r, 3), "\n")
cat("  PC2 vs batch: ", round(pc2_r, 3), "\n")
cat("  Target |r| < 0.2\n")
cat("  Strategy 2 PC1 variance after correction:",
    round(var_after[1], 1), "%\n")
cat("  Record this for comparison with Strategy 1\n")

cat("Block 6 complete ✓\n")
```
